reviewNotes: All 145 reviewed relations are retained and classified exactly once: 84 primary relations in 28 paths and 61 supporting relations in four viewer-oriented groups.

The closed lineage entity vocabulary has no abstract intellectual-work or work-part type. The selection, eighteen item nodes, five related works or compilations, and their relations are therefore retained as explicitly labeled catalog_description projections, never as claims that the abstract works are literal catalog records.

Access rows are omitted for the complete Hilton Scale, Sainte Abbaye, Rolle Canticles, Judica me Deus, and Holy Boke conceptual source works. U-M exposes only selected excerpts, derivatives, or translations; rights for those conceptual nodes have no access_id.
The p02 structural form remains prose although Allen identifies embedded verse-like material encoded inside P; the only two explicit XML L elements are the p17 Thornton closing couplet.
All 272 rollewks page milestones identify Horstmann volume 1. Volume 2 is retained as evidence and review context only; no false consulted or source-carrier graph edge is asserted.

lineage.summary: The repository XML copies the 2003 U-M selection, encoded from Horstmann volume 1. Twenty-eight exact item/source streams produce 84 primary relations; 61 supporting relations preserve item containment, Allen's evaluations, scoped source-work links, facsimiles, catalogs, and p01/p16 collation or attribution evidence. Horstmann volume 2 remains non-relational boundary context because it did not consult the later U-M selection.

catalogingSubject.label: English Prose Works of Richard Rolle: a Selection from the Edition by Carl Horstmann
catalogingSubject.scope_note: A 2003 University of Michigan/CME electronic selection from volume 1 of Carl Horstmann's 1895 Yorkshire Writers edition. It contains eighteen count-once medieval works in fourteen DIV2 containers, including authentic Rolle works, disputed attributions, anonymous translations of Rolle's Latin works, and a Walter Hilton excerpt. The historical collection title must not become blanket authorship metadata.

extent.description: Eighteen count-once works in three editorial groups and fourteen DIV2 containers. All use the repository's prose structural form. The two explicit XML L elements are a closing couplet in p17's Thornton branch. Allen also identifies a ten-line alliterative passage and two lyrics in p02, but CME encodes them inside P, so structural L metrics undercount verse-like material.

formStatements.description: Repository discovery form: prose. Two explicit L lines close p17; p02 contains verse-like passages encoded within P.

notes: The historical title is not blanket Rolle authorship.
Witness dates and regions remain separate from work composition claims.

summaries.value: An edition-defined U-M/CME selection of eighteen Middle English works from Horstmann's 1895 Yorkshire Writers volume 1, preserving accepted Rolle works alongside disputed attributions, a Walter Hilton excerpt, anonymous adaptations, and English translations/compilations over Rolle Latin sources.
Three DIV1 editorial sections contain fourteen DIV2 containers, eighteen count-once works, twenty-one heterogeneous DIV3 nodes, thirty-one DIV4 nodes, and twenty-eight primary source streams.
